enttyp.enttypd: The polygons represent the U.S. Census block groups of the United States. Block Groups (BGs) are statistical divisions of census tracts, are generally defined to contain between 600 and 3,000 people, and are used to present data and control block numbering. A block group consists of clusters of blocks within the same census tract that have the same first digit of their four-digit census block number. For example, blocks 3001, 3002, 3003, thru 3999 in census tract 1210.02 belong to BG 3 in that census tract. A BG usually covers a contiguous area. Each census tract contains at least one BG, and BGs are uniquely numbered within the census tract. Within the standard census geographic hierarchy, BGs never cross state, county, or census tract boundaries, but may cross the boundaries of any other geographic entity. Most BGs are delineated by local participants in the Census Bureau’s Participant Statistical Areas Program (PSAP). The Census Bureau delineates BGs only where a local government declines to participate in PSAP, and a regional organization or the State Data Center is not available to participate.
enttyp.enttypds: U.S. Department of Commerce, Census Bureau

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: The two-letter abbreviation for the state or territory in which the block group is located.
attr.attrdefs: U.S. Department of Commerce, Census Bureau

codesetd.codesetn: INCITS 38-2009, (Formerly FIPS 5-2) Codes for the Identification of the States and Equivalent Areas within the United States, Puerto Rico, and the Insular Areas

attr.attrdef: The code (two-digit number) for the state in which the block group is located.

codesetd.codesetn: INCITS 38-2009, (Formerly FIPS 5-2) Codes for the Identification of the States and Equivalent Areas within the United States, Puerto Rico, and the Insular Areas

attr.attrdef: The code (three-digit number) for the county in which the block group is located.

codesetd.codesetn: INCITS 31-2009, (Formerly FIPS 6-4) Codes for the Identification of Counties and Equivalent Areas of the United States, Puerto Rico, and the Insular Areas

attr.attrdef: The combined state and county codes. County codes begin with 001 for each state; use the combined code (five-digit number) to uniquely identify a county.

codesetd.codesetn: INCITS 38-2009, (Formerly FIPS 5-2) Codes for the Identification of the States and Equivalent Areas within the United States, Puerto Rico, and the Insular Areas; INCITS 31-2009, (Formerly FIPS 6-4) Codes for the Identification of Counties and Equivalent Areas of the United States, Puerto Rico, and the Insular Areas

attr.attrdef: The four-digit number, with a two-digit suffix, used to identify a census tract. Census tract numbers are always unique within a county and usually unique within a metropolitan area.
attr.attrdefs: U.S. Department of Commerce, Census Bureau

attr.attrdef: The Census code (one-digit number) for the block group.
attr.attrdefs: U.S. Department of Commerce, Census Bureau

attr.attrdef: The combined state, county, tract, and block group Census codes. The combined code (12-digit number) provides a unique block group identifier.

codesetd.codesetn: INCITS 38-2009, (Formerly FIPS 5-2) Codes for the Identification of the States and Equivalent Areas within the United States, Puerto Rico, and the Insular Areas; INCITS 31-2009, (Formerly FIPS 6-4) Codes for the Identification of Counties and Equivalent Areas of the United States, Puerto Rico, and the Insular Areas; Federal Information Processing Standards
codesetd.codesets: American National Standards Institute; U.S. Department of Commerce

attr.attrdef: The 2022 population of the block group.

attrdomv.udom: Populations for the features. A <Null> value means no data.

attr.attrdef: The 2022 population of the block group per square mile.

attrdomv.udom: Calculated populations per square mile for the features. A <Null> value means no data.

attr.attrdef: The area of the block group in square miles using the North America Albers Equal Area Conic projection.
The 2020 population of the block group.
attr.attrdefs: U.S. Department of Commerce, Census Bureau

attrdomv.udom: Populations for the features. A <Null> value means no data.

attr.attrdef: The 2020 population of the block group per square mile.
attr.attrdefs: U.S. Department of Commerce, Census Bureau; Esri

attrdomv.udom: Calculated populations per square mile for the features. A <Null> value means no data.

attr.attrdef: Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Area of feature in internal units squared.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

rpCntInfo.cntInstr:
In the United States, contact Esri sales at +1-800-447-9778 or visit https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=1 for more information about our software and data.
Outside the United States, direct all inquiries to your local Esri Office. This information can be found at https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=0.

distorOrdPrc.ordInstr: Data and Maps is available only as part of ArcGIS software.

distorFormat.formatInfo: To use this data requires software that works with FGDB files.

citRespParty.rpOrgName: U.S. Department of Commerce, Census Bureau
U.S. Department of Commerce (DOC), National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), National Ocean Service (NOS), National Geodetic Survey (NGS)

dataIdInfo.idAbs: U.S. Census Block Group Boundaries represents the 2020 U.S. Census block groups of the United States in the 50 states and the District of Columbia. Block Groups are polygons containing population totals from the 2020 Census and Esri Updated Demographics.
dataIdInfo.idPurp: U.S. Census Block Group Boundaries provides the boundaries and 2020 U.S. Census codes and population information for the Census block groups of the United States. The boundaries are consistent with the county, state, and Census tract datasets.

rpCntInfo.cntInstr:
In the United States– Direct all inquiries regarding software and data pricing and consulting services to your local Esri Regional Office. For support, contact Esri by telephone (voice) between 5:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m. Pacific time, Monday through Friday, by dialing +1-888-377-4575; facsimile (fax) available at +1-909-792-0960; or visit https://support.esri.com/; Esri holidays excluded. Email and chat are available through the Support page on My Esri (https://my.esri.com).
Outside the United States– Direct all inquiries regarding software and data pricing, sales, support, and consulting services to your local Esri Office. This information can be found at https://www.esri.com/en-us/contact#c=us&t=0. For other questions or comments, you may contact Esri headquarters by email, telephone, or fax or write to us.

LegConsts.othConsts:
The data are provided by multiple, third party data vendors under license to Esri for inclusion on ArcGIS® Data and Maps for use with ArcGIS software. Each data vendor has its own data licensing policies and may grant varying redistribution rights to end users. Consult the redistribution rights below for this dataset provided on Data and Maps. As used herein, “Geodata” shall mean any digital dataset consisting of geographic data coordinates and associated attributes.
The redistribution rights for this dataset: Redistribution rights are granted by the data vendor for hard-copy renditions or static, electronic map images (for example, .gif, .jpeg, and so on) that are plotted, printed, or publicly displayed with proper metadata and source/copyright attribution to the respective data vendor(s). Geodata is redistributable with a Value-Added Software Application developed by Esri Business Partners on a royalty-free basis with proper metadata and source/copyright attribution to the respective data vendor(s). Geodata is redistributable without a Value-Added Software Application (such as, adding the sample data to an existing, [non]commercial dataset for redistribution) with proper metadata and source/copyright attribution to the respective data vendor(s).
The terms and conditions below apply to all the datasets provided on Data and Maps.
Proprietary Rights and Copyright: Licensee acknowledges that the Data and Related Materials contain proprietary and confidential property of Esri and its licensor(s). The Data and Related Materials are owned by Esri and its licensor(s) and are protected by United States copyright laws and applicable international copyright treaties and/or conventions.
Limited Warranty and Disclaimer: Esri warrants that the media upon which the Data and Related Materials are provided will be free from defects in materials and labor under normal use and service for a period of ninety (90) days from the date of receipt.
THE DATA AND RELATED MATERIALS ARE EXCLUDED FROM THE LIMITED WARRANTY, AND THE LICENSEE EXPRESSLY ACKNOWLEDGES THAT THE DATA CONTAINS SOME NONCONFORMITIES, DEFECTS, OR ERRORS. ESRI DOES NOT WARRANT THAT THE DATA WILL MEET LICENSEE'S NEEDS OR EXPECTATIONS; THAT THE USE OF THE DATA WILL BE UNINTERRUPTED; OR THAT ALL NONCONFORMITIES, DEFECTS, OR ERRORS CAN OR WILL BE CORRECTED. ESRI IS NOT INVITING RELIANCE ON THIS DATA, AND THE LICENSEE SHOULD ALWAYS VERIFY ACTUAL DATA.
EXCEPT FOR THE LIMITED WARRANTY SET FORTH ABOVE, THE DATA AND RELATED MATERIALS CONTAINED THEREIN ARE PROVIDED "AS-IS," WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EITHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO, THE IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.
HIGH RISK ACTIVITIES: (A) THE DATA AND RELATED MATERIALS ARE NOT FAULT-TOLERANT AND ARE NOT DESIGNED, MANUFACTURED, OR INTENDED FOR USE OR RESALE FOR INSURANCE UNDERWRITING OR WITH CRITICAL HEALTH AND SAFETY OR ONLINE CONTROL EQUIPMENT IN HAZARDOUS ENVIRONMENTS THAT REQUIRE FAIL-SAFE PERFORMANCE, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE OPERATION OF NUCLEAR FACILITIES, NAVIGATION OR GUIDANCE (SUCH AS, AIRCRAFT, NAUTICAL VEHICLE, AND SO ON), OR COMMUNICATION SYSTEMS, AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL, EMERGENCY RESPONSE, TERRORISM PREVENTION OR RESPONSE, LIFE SUPPORT, OR WEAPONS SYSTEMS ("HIGH RISK ACTIVITIES"). ESRI SPECIFICALLY DISCLAIMS ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTY OF FITNESS FOR HIGH RISK ACTIVITIES.
(B) TO THE EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, LICENSEE AGREES TO INDEMNIFY, DEFEND, AND HOLD ESRI, ITS OFFICERS, DIRECTORS, EMPLOYEES, AGENTS, SUBCONTRACTORS, LICENSORS, SUCCESSORS, AND ASSIGNS HARMLESS FROM AND AGAINST ANY AND ALL LIABILITY, LOSSES, CLAIMS, EXPENSES (INCLUDING ATTORNEYS' FEES), DEMANDS, OR DAMAGES OF ANY KIND, INCLUDING DIRECT, INDIRECT, SPECIAL, PUNITIVE, INCIDENTAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES, ARISING OUT OF OR IN ANY WAY CONNECTED WITH LICENSEE'S USE OR PERMITTING THE USE BY OTHERS OF THE DATA, AND VENDOR'S HARDWARE OR SOFTWARE FOR HIGH RISK ACTIVITIES. DELIVERY OF THE DATA, AND VENDOR'S HARDWARE OR SOFTWARE DOES NOT CONSTITUTE A WAIVER OF THE RIGHTS AND OBLIGATIONS SET FORTH IN THIS ARTICLE.
Exclusive Remedy and Limitation of Liability: The entire liability of Esri or its licensor(s) and Licensee's exclusive remedy shall be to terminate the Agreement upon Licensee returning the Data and Related Materials to Esri with a copy of Licensee's invoice/receipt and Esri returning the license fees paid to Licensee.
IN NO EVENT SHALL ESRI AND/OR ITS LICENSOR(S) BE LIABLE FOR COSTS OF PROCUREMENT OF SUBSTITUTE GOODS OR SERVICES; LOST PROFITS, LOST SALES, OR BUSINESS EXPENDITURES, INVESTMENTS, OR COMMITMENTS IN CONNECTION WITH ANY BUSINESS; LOSS OF ANY GOODWILL; OR FOR ANY INDIRECT, SPECIAL, INCIDENTAL, EXEMPLARY, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT OR USE OF THE DATA AND RELATED MATERIALS, HOWEVER CAUSED, ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY, AND WHETHER OR NOT ESRI OR ITS LICENSOR(S) HAVE BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE. THESE LIMITATIONS SHALL APPLY NOTWITHSTANDING ANY FAILURE OF ESSENTIAL PURPOSE OF ANY EXCLUSIVE REMEDY.
Third Party Beneficiary: Esri's licensor(s) has (have) authorized Esri to (sub)distribute and (sub)license its (their) data as incorporated into the Data and Related Materials. As an intended third party beneficiary to this Agreement, the Esri licensor(s) is (are) entitled to directly enforce, in its own name, the rights and obligations undertaken by the Licensee and to seek all legal and equitable remedies as are afforded to Esri.
In the event that the data vendor(s) has (have) granted the end user permission to redistribute the Geodata, use proper proprietary or copyright attribution for the various data vendor(s), and provide the associated metadata file(s) with the Geodata. In compliance with FGDC metadata standards, Esri has attempted to practice proper metadata methodologies by providing any data source information, descriptions, and file names to assist in this effort.

report.measDesc: The dataset is converted to file geodatabase (FGDB) format using tools in ArcGIS. The geometry is checked, and if needed repaired.
After processing, the dataset is checked for drawing display and number of records and file sizes compared with source materials.

The spatial accuracy varies with the source materials used. The Census Bureau cannot specify the spatial accuracy of features changed or added by field staff or through local updates, features derived from the GBF/DIME Files (TIGER’s predecessor in 1970 and 1980), or other map or digital sources. The positional accuracy is unknown.

srcCitatn.resTitle: National Sub-State Geography Geodatabase; Areal Hydrography National Geodatabase, Linear Hydrography National Geodatabase

citRespParty.rpOrgName: U.S. Department of Commerce, Census Bureau
U.S. Department of Commerce, Census Bureau

datasetSeries.seriesName: Census Redistricting Data (Public Law 94-171) Summary File

srcCitatn.otherCitDet: The initial source of this data is the 2020 Census Redistricting Data (Public Law 94-171) Summary File.

citRespParty.rpOrgName: U.S. Department of Commerce, Census Bureau
National Ocean Service (NOS), National Geodetic Survey (NGS)
U.S. Department of Commerce (DOC), National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), National Ocean Service (NOS), National Geodetic Survey (NGS)

prcStep.stepDesc: The following steps were performed by Esri: Downloaded Census TIGER/Line Geodatabases 2020 from the U.S. Census Bureau and extracted the block groups dataset. Clipped to the shoreline using Census TIGER/Line Hydrography Geodatabases 2020 and NOAA National Shoreline datasets. Projected the dataset to World Geodetic System 1984 (WGS84). Added the field STATE_ABBR from Census TIGER/Line Geodatabases 2020. Added the fields STATE_FIPS, COUNTY_FIPS, STCOFIPS, TRACT_FIPS, and BLOCKGROUP_FIPS and created their values from parsing the values of the FIPS source field. Added the field POPULATION (alias POPULATION_2020) from Esri and U.S. Census Bureau: 2020 (Public Law 94-171). Added the field SQMI and calculated its values projected to North America Albers Equal Area Conic. Added and calculated the field POP_SQMI (alias POP20_SQMI). Reordered and renamed fields.

stepSrc.srcDesc: Sources: 1) Census TIGER/Line Geodatabases 2020; 2) Esri and U.S. Census Bureau: 2020 (PL 94-171); and 3) NOAA National Shoreline.

prcStep.stepDesc: The following steps were performed by Esri: Renamed field POPULATION to POPULATION_2020 and field POP_SQMI to POP20_SQMI and moved them to the end of the list of fields. Added the field POPULATION (alias POPULATION_2022) and added and calculated the field POP_SQMI (alias POP22_SQMI).
